mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.2 percent within a single month, it signals a labor market inflection that triggers recession fears, compressing equity multiples and driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in real economic activity, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and depresses equity prices over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-interest-rate environment, rising consumer credit balances indicate resilient household spending that offsets tight financial conditions, supporting positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The positive return prediction reverses when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.5 percent, or forward returns show no statistical difference between high-credit and low-credit subsamples (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating quarterly real GDP growth expands the earnings denominator for S&P 500 constituents, generating positive forward equity returns over the subsequent trading week as analysts revise estimates upward.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth decelerates while SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of observations, or the rolling 1-year Spearman correlation between GDP growth and 5-day returns falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls exceed 200,000 for two consecutive months while the unemployment rate remains below 4.0 percent, it confirms a wage-price spiral risk that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, weighing on equities.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS > 200000 for 2 consecutive months AND UNRATE < 4.0
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During supply chain normalization phases, rising industrial production combined with stable inflation reduces input cost volatility, allowing corporate margins to expand and driving positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly higher when INDPRO accelerates and CPIAUCSL remains flat, or the predictive power vanishes when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5 percent year-over-year.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust labor demand that supports consumer spending and corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Monthly payroll growth remains positive while SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of observations, or the rolling 1-year Rank IC drops below 0.02.
